ingredients.name: Lactobacillus plantarum 299v
ingredients.notes: Lactobacillus plantarum — versatile probiotic species with strong GI colonization. Specific strain (LP299v, DR7) yields higher scoring.

inactive_ingredients.name: Potato Starch
inactive_ingredients.notes: Natural starch from potatoes, used as a binder and thickener. Gluten-free and generally hypoallergenic.

inactive_ingredients.name: Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose
inactive_ingredients.notes: Generic capsule delivery format descriptor. Not a specific ingredient but a formulation type. Classified as non-active to prevent unmapped inflation.

inactive_ingredients.name: Magnesium Stearate
inactive_ingredients.notes: Magnesium stearate is an FDA GRAS flow agent present in the vast majority of supplement tablets and capsules. It prevents powders from sticking to manufacturing equipment. Internet claims that it blocks nutrient absorption are not supported by clinical evidence — the single study cited used unrealistic concentrations. Stearic acid (its main component) is a natural fatty acid found in chocolate, meat, and dairy. Penalized at low severity only as a non-nutritive processing aid, not as a health concern.
inactive_ingredients.mechanism_of_harm: FDA GRAS flow agent (lubricant) used in virtually all tablet and capsule manufacturing. Facilitates powder flow through equipment. Claims of bioavailability reduction are based on a single in-vitro dissolution study (Brittain 2002) at unrealistically high concentrations; multiple subsequent studies found no meaningful effect on absorption at typical 0.5-2% levels. Stearic acid is a common dietary saturated fat found in cocoa butter and meat.
population_warnings: No specific population warnings — safe at typical supplement levels
inactive_ingredients.harmful_notes: FDA GRAS flow agent (lubricant) used in virtually all tablet and capsule manufacturing. Facilitates powder flow through equipment. Stearic acid is a common dietary saturated fat found in cocoa butter and meat.

warnings.title: Contains Magnesium Stearate
warnings.detail: FDA GRAS flow agent (lubricant) used in virtually all tablet and capsule manufacturing. Facilitates powder flow through equipment. Stearic acid is a common dietary saturated fat found in cocoa butter and meat.

Internet claims that it blocks nutrient absorption are not supported by clinical evidence — the single study cited used unrealistic concentrations. Stearic acid (its main component) is a natural fatty acid found in chocolate, meat, and dairy. Penalized at low severity only as a non-nutritive processing aid, not as a health concern.
warnings.mechanism_of_harm: FDA GRAS flow agent (lubricant) used in virtually all tablet and capsule manufacturing. Facilitates powder flow through equipment. population_warnings: No specific population warnings — safe at typical supplement levels
warnings.safety_summary: A widely-used flow agent in supplement manufacturing. Claims of harm at the amounts used are not supported by evidence — low overall concern.
warnings.safety_summary_one_liner: Common flow agent — low overall concern.

warnings.title: Allergen: Soy & Soy Lecithin
warnings.notes: FDA major allergen (FALCPA). Soy lecithin is the most common form in supplements, used as emulsifier in softgels and powders. Highly refined soy oil may not trigger reactions but must still be labeled. Cross-reactivity with other legumes (peanut, lupin) occurs in 5-10% of soy-allergic individuals. Fermented soy (natto) retains allergenic proteins.
warnings.supplement_context: Common emulsifier/excipient and in protein products.
